name: Perry Noble
role: Founder and Former Senior Pastor, NewSpring Church, Anderson, South Carolina
incident: Perry Noble, founder of NewSpring Church which grew to 32,000 members under his leadership, was fired by the church's board of directors in July 2016 for alcohol abuse and what the board described as the related effects on his marriage. Noble had preached extensively about personal discipline, accountability, and the destructive effects of addiction from his pulpit.

name: James MacDonald
role: Founder and Former Senior Pastor, Harvest Bible Chapel, Rolling Meadows, Illinois
incident: James MacDonald, founder of Harvest Bible Chapel which grew to 13,000 members, was fired by the church's elders on February 12, 2019 after recordings surfaced in which he made vulgar statements about colleagues and a journalist investigating him. Subsequent investigations documented that MacDonald had pressured church staff to make fraudulent insurance claims, improperly used church funds for personal expenses including a hunting ranch, and led a pattern of bullying and spiritual abuse spanning years.

name: Eddie Long
role: Senior Pastor, New Birth Missionary Baptist Church, Lithonia, Georgia
incident: Bishop Eddie Long, senior pastor of New Birth Missionary Baptist Church with a congregation of 25,000 and a prominent voice against same-sex marriage, faced lawsuits filed on September 22, 2010 by four young men alleging he had used spiritual authority and gifts to coerce them into sexual relationships beginning when they were teenagers. Long had been a leading figure in organizing a march against same-sex marriage in Atlanta in 2004.
